lineage.mechanism: stocks with lower trailing market beta may provide a more defensive large-cap equity portfolio without relying on momentum, fundamentals, optimization, leverage, or shorting

research_question: Can a fixed quarterly portfolio of the lowest-beta stocks in the frozen current Top-50-derived universe improve risk-adjusted performance and drawdown versus SPY while retaining broadly comparable CAGR and low turnover?
null_hypothesis: The strategy fails at least one frozen discovery gate against SPY or the same-date eligible-stock equal-weight benchmark.

universe.scope: the same 46 current Top-50-derived qualified US stocks frozen by the input identity
universe.secondary_benchmark: equal weight all beta-eligible qualified stocks at each formation

signal.formation_session: last SPY trading session of each formation month
signal.beta_model: OLS slope of stock daily total return on intercept and SPY daily total return
signal.beta_estimation_window: 252 daily returns ending on the formation close inclusive
signal.ranking: ascending beta with ascending symbol tie-break

portfolio.weighting: equal weight among the 15 selected stocks
portfolio.entry: next SPY trading session close after formation
portfolio.holding_period: until the next calendar-quarterly execution close

costs.turnover_definition: 0.5 times the sum of absolute target-weight changes; initial entry excluded from annual turnover but charged costs

forbidden_changes: change the 252-session beta window or 220-observation minimum
change selected count or retention buffer
change weighting, costs, benchmarks, or gates
try alternative low-volatility, beta, quality, momentum, or market-state variants
add leverage, shorting, an optimizer, or new Python dependencies

conditional_pit_validation.required_upgrade: historical point-in-time Top-50 membership or an explicitly frozen reproducible PIT market-cap reconstruction
